title: The 25 Best Movies of the Century: No. 1 - 'The Social Network'
format: The finale of the 25-for-25 countdown: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins on why David Fincher and Aaron Sorkin's 'The Social Network' lands at No. 1, plus the Fincher films it beat and a tour of the Academy's worst Best Picture calls.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: The No. 1 pick, and the hosts say there was never any debate about the slot. Fincher directing a Sorkin script off Ben Mezrich's 'The Accidental Billionaires'; $225M on a $40M budget, a clean 120 minutes. Framed as their generation's 'Citizen Kane': a tech mogul who builds an empire while alienating everyone, ending alone refreshing a friend request. Jesse Eisenberg as an already-sour Mark Zuckerberg (not a heartbroken one), Andrew Garfield's Eduardo as the lone empathetic center, Justin Timberlake's Sean Parker weaponizing his own celebrity. The Reznor/Ross score is called a turning point for film music; won adapted screenplay, editing, and score. Lost Best Picture to 'The King's Speech' and Fincher lost director to Tom Hooper.

segments.heading: The Fincher pick
segments.note: which Fincher belongs on the list

films.title: Zodiac
films.note: No. 19 on the countdown. The proposed trade: Sean offered Zodiac over Social Network in exchange for One Battle over There Will Be Blood; Amanda refused. Called Fincher's most representative film: longer, more discursive, unresolved about obsession itself.

films.title: Gone Girl
films.note: No. 64. A distant third among the Fincher contenders but still 'a great movie we love,' sharp about celebrity, marriage, and what couples perform in public.

films.title: Panic Room
films.note: Named in passing as a 'very slick, cool thriller.'

films.title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
films.note: The Oscar-nominated left turn Fincher made just before this: a mastery-of-form film reaching for emotion he can't quite access.

films.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
films.note: 'Banger.' An underrated Christmas movie in Sean's telling.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Fincher's sentimental side; Amanda thinks it's bad, but wonders what a Sorkin co-write would have done with it.

films.title: The Killer
films.note: 'This rules.' Loved, but never a contender for the top spot.

segments.heading: The Academy's worst calls
segments.note: the Best Picture and director upsets this loss belongs beside

films.title: The King's Speech
films.note: Beat 'The Social Network' for Best Picture. 'Not a bad film, just not really anything interesting.'

films.title: The Artist
films.note: Won the very next year; the hosts read the back-to-back as the low point of the Weinstein era.

films.title: Black Swan
films.note: Aronofsky, one of the four bigger auteurs Tom Hooper beat for director.

films.title: The Fighter
films.note: David O. Russell, also nominated for director that year.

films.title: True Grit
films.note: The Coens, rounding out the director field Hooper won over.

films.title: How Green Was My Valley
films.note: Beat 'Citizen Kane': the canonical bad win the show cites, though a film you can 'kind of see.'

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: The touchstone throughout: Social Network as 'Citizen Kane if Kane went on trial in the middle of the movie.'

films.title: Forrest Gump
films.note: Won over 'Shawshank' and 'Pulp Fiction.' Sean's on the record liking it; still a bad win.

films.title: Pulp Fiction
films.note: A new form of filmmaking that lost to 'Forrest Gump.'

films.title: Dances with Wolves
films.note: Over 'Goodfellas': a tough one you can at least see the case for.

films.title: Shakespeare in Love
films.note: Over 'Saving Private Ryan': the most defensible upset on the list, per Amanda, especially after 'Hamnet.'

films.title: Crash
films.note: Over 'Brokeback Mountain,' called just as indefensible as the Fincher loss. 'Crash sucks.'

films.title: Brokeback Mountain
films.note: A borderline masterpiece, No. 27 or 28 on their countdown; should have won.

films.title: Schindler's List
films.note: One of only three films to sweep the Big Four critics awards, and it did win Best Picture.

films.title: L.A. Confidential
films.note: Swept the Big Four critics awards but did not win Best Picture; the other precedent for Social Network's sweep.

The Curious Case of Benjamin Button.title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button

The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
